programme.title: The Casagrandes: Karma Chameleon/ Team Effort
programme.desc: The Changs go on vacation and leave Ronnie Anne and her cousins to watch after Cam, the chameleon snake.

programme.title: The Loud House: The Mad Scientist/ Missed Connection
programme.desc: Lisa gets invited to work at an institute and get away from her crazy family. Lori and Bobby worry that dating long-distance is causing them to lose their connection.

programme.title: The Loud House: Sitting Bull/ The Spies Who Loved Me
programme.desc: The older sisters reluctantly let Lynn into their babysitting club. Ronnie Anne wants to go downtown, but her grandparents think it's too dangerous.

programme.title: SpongeBob SquarePants: New Student Starfish/ Clams
programme.desc: Pat joins SpongeBob at boating school; Mr. Krabs takes Bob and Squid fishing.

programme.title: SpongeBob SquarePants: Patrick's Staycation/ Walking the Plankton
programme.desc: Patrick wants to take a vacation, but can't afford one, so SpongeBob comes up with a solution: a staycation.

programme.title: SpongeBob SquarePants: Spongebob BC
programme.desc: Thanks to the magic of an underwater thunderstorm, our neanderthal friends discover fire.

programme.title: It's Pony: Horace/The Boot
programme.desc: Annie can avoid detention if she can heal the principal's dying plant, but the plan goes awry when Pony becomes attached to it. Pony's half-baked plan causes Annie to lose one of her boots, which she desperately needs for a family photo!

programme.title: SpongeBob SquarePants: Ghoul Fools
programme.desc: SpongeBob and Patrick get caught in the feud between a crew of ghost pirates and the Flying Dutchman.

programme.title: SpongeBob SquarePants: The Great Snail Race/ Mid-Life Crustacean
programme.desc: Squidward races a purebred snail; Mr. Krabs follows Pat and Bob out on the town.

programme.title: SpongeBob SquarePants: Fiasco!
programme.desc: Plankton thinks he's stolen a Krabby Patty from the Krusty Krab, but he's really stolen an art piece by the famous artist, Fiasco.

programme.title: SpongeBob SquarePants: Krabby Land/ The Camping Episode
programme.desc: Mr. Krabs builds a children's theme park; Pat and SpongeBob camp in the backyard.

programme.title: SpongeBob SquarePants: Extreme Sports/ Squirrel Record
programme.desc: SpongeBob and Patrick try to join the extreme sports team the Drasticals. Also, Sandy finds a book of world's records and tries to break them all with SpongeBob's help.

programme.title: SpongeBob SquarePants: The Lost Episode
programme.desc: Spongebob is jellyfishing when he realizes that he wants to fly with the jellyfish.

programme.title: SpongeBob SquarePants: Don't Look Now/ Séance Schméance
programme.desc: SpongeBob holds a séance at the Krusty Krab to learn the recipe for a long-lost sandwich. Then, SpongeBob and Patrick go to see the latest horror film, only to be seemingly stalked by the villain.

programme.title: SpongeBob SquarePants: Fear of a Krabby Patty/ Shell of a Man
programme.desc: SpongeBob develops a fear of Krabby Patties; Mr. Krab molts his shell before a reunion.

programme.title: SpongeBob SquarePants: All That Glitters/ Wishing You Well
programme.desc: SpongeBob's spatula breaks mid-patty flip; Mr. Krabs installs a wishing well near the Krusty Krab and sends SpongeBob down the well in a bucket to collect the coins.

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles: The Power Inside Her
programme.desc: A concerned Donnie tests April to discover her psychic abilities are rapidly growing in power.

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles: Tokka vs. the World
programme.desc: Raph's bonding with little Chompy is interrupted by the invasion of Tokka, who has come to Earth searching for him.

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles: Tale of the Tiger Claw
programme.desc: A mysterious new enemy with superhuman speed is on the hunt for Tiger Claw, and will take out whoever stands in her way.

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles: Darkest Plight
programme.desc: The Turtle team hunts for their Sensei, but soon realize they are being hunted as well.

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles
programme.desc: Leonardo, Donatello, Raphael, Michelangelo, and Splinter work to keep the city safe.

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles: Scroll of the Demodragon
programme.desc: The Turtles believe they have defeated their old enemies, but they soon discover a new evil is rising.

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles: The Forgotten Swordsmen
programme.desc: Karai searches for the Kuro Kabuto, claiming to be the rightful heir to the Foot Clan, when she encounters an old rival.

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles: Heart of Evil
programme.desc: Donnie's grudge against Don Vizioso threatens the Turtles' mission.

programme.title: ALVINNN!!! and the Chipmunks: Super Heroes/ She's Got Style
programme.desc: When Theodore thinks he's a real superhero, Alvin and Simon have a tough time convincing him he's not. Brittany is embarrassed by Jeanette's lack of style until Jeanette is proclaimed a fashion trendsetter by Brittany's icon.

programme.title: ALVINNN!!! and the Chipmunks: Mutiny/ Reality or Not
programme.desc: Desperate to avenge last year's loss, The Chipmunks argue over who is best suited to lead them to victory. Later, Alvin is tired of seeing his celebrity status overshadowed by Reality TV stars. So, he decides to become one.

programme.title: ALVINNN!!! and the Chipmunks: Members Only/ The New Kid
programme.desc: Alvin helps the mathletes stand up to the school bullies, but in the process they become bullies themselves. Alvin befriends a lonely new kid in school, but Theodore discovers the new student may not be who he says he is.

programme.title: ALVINNN!!! and the Chipmunks: Good Luck Mr. Whiskers/ Who's Your Daddy
programme.desc: Theodore is convinced that Miss Croner's missing cat is the cause of a terrible curse. When Dave comes down with a bad cold, Alvin quickly offers to take over Dad duty.

programme.title: ALVINNN!!! and the Chipmunks: Albrittina/ Simon for President
programme.desc: When Alvin catches Simon taking a test to discover his soul mate, he decides to do the same and prove just how silly the test really is. Simon and Alvin decide to rebel and launch their own campaigns for class president.

programme.title: Fairly OddParents: Mighty Mom and Dyno Dad Meet Crimson Chin
programme.desc: When Timmy accidentally releases the crimson chin's arch-villain, all heck breaks loose.

programme.title: Fairly OddParents: Most Wanted Wish/ This Is Your Wish
programme.desc: Timmy wishes he was the most wanted kid in America; Mamma Cosmo is back.

programme.title: Fairly OddParents: Beddy Bye/ The Grass Is Always Greener
programme.desc: Timmy always falls asleep at 9 p.m., so he wishes for a world without sleep, much to the chagrin of the human race and the Sandman. Timmy runs away to become a carnie.

programme.title: Fairly OddParents: Secrety Origin of Denzel Crocker
programme.desc: Every March 15th, Denzel Crocker reaches new heights in terror. Timmy has had enough and goes back in time to discover it's the date Crocker lost his fairies when he was a kid.

programme.title: Fairly OddParents: Kung Timmy/ Which Witch is Which?
programme.desc: Timmy wishes that he had amazing kung-fu powers; Timmy is almost burned at the stake.

programme.title: The Thundermans: Why You Buggin'?
programme.desc: After discovering that Link's ex-girlfriend is a secret supervillain who wants Link for herself, Phoebe enlists Max's help in exposing her rival's true motives.

programme.title: The Thundermans: Aunt Misbehavin'
programme.desc: Max and Phoebe give their mom the ultimate birthday surprise by tracking down her long lost sister, but the gift goes awry when they discover these sisters have been feuding for years.

programme.title: Big City Greens: Trailer Trouble/ Mansion Madness
programme.desc: Nancy's trailer is stolen by her old biker gang, and the kids resolve to get it back. Cricket house-sits for Remy, but things go awry when the rest of the Greens visit.

programme.title: Big City Greens: Park Pandemonium/ Cricket's Biscuits
programme.desc: Follow the adventures of a boy whose curiosity and enthusiasm lead him on journeys.

programme.title: Big City Greens: Skunked/ Axin' Saxon
programme.desc: Cricket stages a crisis by letting skunks into the café, but it gets out of control. Nancy accidentally destroys Saxon, but Tilly mistakenly directs her wrath at Gramma.

programme.title: Big City Greens: Cricket's Place/ Volunteer Tilly
programme.desc: Follow the adventures of a boy whose curiosity and enthusiasm lead him on journeys.

programme.title: Big City Greens: Cricket's Kapowie! / Car Trouble
programme.desc: Cricket earns a role in Big Coffee’s new TV commercial but loses his confidence. Bill trades his pickup in for a cutting-edge electric car, but gets more than he bargained for.

programme.title: Big City Greens: Quiet Please/ Chipwrecked
programme.desc: When the Greens visit the library, the librarian threatens to throw them out if they make a sound. Chip Whistler attempts to make Cricket lose his job by running Big Coffee out of business.

programme.title: Toy Story That Time Forgot
programme.desc: During a post-Christmas play date, the gang finds themselves in uncharted territory when the coolest set of action figures ever turn out to be dangerously delusional. It's all up to Trixie the triceratops if the gang hopes to return to Bonnie's room.

programme.title: DuckTales: How Santa Stole Christmas!
programme.desc: Donald Duck and his nephews are reunited with Scrooge after moving into McDuck Manor.

programme.title: DuckTales: Last Christmas!
programme.desc: Scrooge celebrates Christmas with his time-traveling ghost buddies while time-lost Dewey teams with Young Donald to find his mom on Christmas.

programme.title: Big City Greens: Green Christmas
programme.desc: Cricket becomes worried that he's not on Santa's 'Nice List' so he goes on a spree of good deeds. Gramma and Nancy go on a last minute search for Bill's gift.

programme.title: Oddbods: The Festive Menace
programme.desc: T'was the night before Christmas and trouble is brewing, Santa's gone bad and it's the Oddbods doing.

programme.title: Phineas and Ferb: Phineas and Ferb Christmas Vacation!
programme.desc: Phineas and Ferb want to show Santa how grateful they are for the work that he does every year at Christmas time by transforming the city of Danville into a giant shimmering thank you card and building a rest stop for Santa to relax in.

programme.title: Toy Story That Time Forgot
programme.desc: During a post-Christmas play date, the gang finds themselves in uncharted territory when the coolest set of action figures ever turn out to be dangerously delusional. It's all up to Trixie the triceratops if the gang hopes to return to Bonnie's room.

programme.title: Phineas and Ferb: S'Winter
programme.desc: Two stepbrothers build various objects with materials found around their home.

programme.title: Kick Buttowski Suburban Daredevil: A Cousin Kyle Christmas/ Snow Problem
programme.desc: Kick battles an ice storm and bizarre obstacles in order to find a last minute Christmas gift for Cousin Kyle. When Kick's family gets snowed in from an avalanche, Kick goes to the extreme to rescue them before cabin fever sets in.

programme.title: Kick Buttowski Suburban Daredevil: Snowpolcalypse!/ According to Chimp
programme.desc: Kick causes the school bus to get trapped during a blizzard. Later, Kick brings home a lost chimp named Dr. Awesome and makes it his new pet. Kick soon realizes that the apparently cute little primate becomes destructive without chocolate bananas.

programme.title: Kick Buttowski Suburban Daredevil: If Books Could Kill/ There Will Be Nachos
programme.desc: Kick challenged a library's return policy after Gunther accidentally brings back a book signed by Kick's idol Billy Stumps. Kick is then determined to crash a party held by Brad, even though he was not invited, in an attempt to score some nachos.

programme.title: Kick Buttowski Suburban Daredevil: Brad's Room/ Dude, Where's My Wade?
programme.desc: When Kick's room is destroyed, Dad grounds Kick and makes him move into Brad's room where he must abide by his older brother's ridiculous rules. Kick and Gunther follow clues to track down Wade when he goes missing before the safety inspector's visit.

programme.title: Kick Buttowski Suburban Daredevil: For the Love of Gunther/ Father From the Truth
programme.desc: Kick trains his best friend to be a stuntman when Gunther reveals he has a crush.

programme.title: Kick Buttowski Suburban Daredevil: Sleepy River Wild/ Power Play
programme.desc: Gunther falls asleep on a waterpark ride/ Kick tries to get out of playing Romeo.

programme.title: Kick Buttowski Suburban Daredevil: Clean... to the Extreme/ Stand and Delivery
programme.desc: Kick accidentally breaks Gunther's dad's foosball table and must complete a series of favours to replace it before Gunther's dad gets home. Kick takes over the delivery services at Battlesnax and must defend delivery guarantee.

programme.title: Kick Buttowski Suburban Daredevil: Bwar and Peace
programme.desc: Kick and Gunther venture to the Old Country where Gunther is mistaken for a warrior.

programme.title: Kick Buttowski Suburban Daredevil: Meathead Justice/ Bwar-Mart
programme.desc: After Kick saves the DiPazzi twins' lives, they become indebted to him by their code of ethics. When they do not leave his side, he must go to extreme measures to get them out of his life.

programme.title: Kick Buttowski Suburban Daredevil: Big Mouth/ Last Fan Standing
programme.desc: Kick can't get a wink of sleep when Wacky Jackie and Cousin Kyle throw him a celebration in honour of his latest stunt.

programme.title: Kick Buttowski Suburban Daredevil: Stumped Again/ The Kick Stays in the Picture
programme.desc: Kick must choose between helping his idol Billy Stumps in a monster truck show and helping his best friend Gunther with an important Viking coming-of-age ritual. Kick teams up with Jackie to take a photo for a billboard contest.

programme.title: Kick Buttowski Suburban Daredevil: Abandon Friendship!/ Braking the Grade
programme.desc: When Kick and Gunther's parents have a fight, the boys fear their friendship is over and go out to find new friends. Then, fearing he's bound to get bad grades, Kick does everything he can to keep his report card from his dad.

programme.title: Kick Buttowski Suburban Daredevil: You've Been Brad'd/ Sleepover
programme.desc: After Kick discovers that he is the sole target of Brad's new Internet prank show, he must find a way to avoid his brother and complete an impossible stunt.

programme.title: Kick Buttowski Suburban Daredevil: Kickasaurus Wrecks/ Battle for the 'Snax
programme.desc: Kick is forced into a shrimp costume after being hired to perform during the halftime show for a local football team. Gunther's parents are forced to sell their beloved restaurant and move back to the old country unless Kick can help them get customers.

programme.title: Phineas and Ferb: Great Balls of Water/ Where's Pinky?
programme.desc: When Candace thinks Jeremy is bored with their usual hangouts, she sets out to find something unique to do with him. When Pinky goes missing, Phineas and Ferb create a machine that amplifies Buford's sense to those of a dog's.

programme.title: Phineas and Ferb: The Lizard Whisperer/ Robot Rodeo
programme.desc: After being inadvertently zapped by a 'gigantinator' ray, Phineas and Ferb's chameleon grows to the size of a dinosaur. Candace and Stacy are upset because Stacy's mom does not think they are responsible enough to go to a concert on their own.

programme.title: Phineas and Ferb: Skiddley Whiffers/ Tour de Ferb
programme.desc: The boys build a giant version of a childhood game, using Danville as the central location. Candace ends up playing along with them so that she can carry on her title of 'Skiddley Whiffers Queen.' Doofenshmirtz tries to deal with Vanessa growing up.

programme.title: Phineas and Ferb: For Your Ice Only/ Happy New Year
programme.desc: NHL superstar Luc Robitaille offers his assistance when Phineas and Ferb take an ice hockey game to the extreme. Phineas and Ferb plan their own New Year's Eve ball drop, complete with a customized multi-dimensional ball, while Candice makes a resolution.

programme.title: Beyblade Burst Rise: Ultimate Creation! Eclipse Genesis!
Pokémon: Sun & Moon - Ultra Adventures: League Offenders and Defenders!
Gravity Falls: Tourist Trapped
programme.desc: Dipper and Mabel arrive to spend the summer in Gravity Falls and soon discover that something is amiss with the remote town.

programme.title: Gravity Falls: The Legend of the Gobblewonker
programme.desc: The twins embark on a mission to prove that a legendary sea monster exists at the bottom of the local lake. Soos helps them on a boating mission to prove it exists.

programme.title: Gravity Falls: Headhunters
programme.desc: When someone tampers with Mabel's life-size wax statue, she and Dipper set out to find the culprit. Meanwhile, Grunkle Stan mourns over the loss of his wax doppelganger.

programme.title: Gravity Falls: The Inconveniencing
programme.desc: When Dipper tries to impress Wendy by acting older than he really is, he's invited to join a group of teenagers on a trek that leads to an old abandoned convenience store that could be haunted.

programme.title: Gravity Falls: The Hand That Rocks the Mabel
programme.desc: Dipper and Mabel discover their seemingly cherubic new neighbour, Lil' Gideon, happens to be the town's adored psychic. But when Dipper convinces Mabel to go on a date with Gideon to question his authenticity, she soon learns that his cuteness can fade.

programme.title: Gravity Falls: Dipper vs. Manliness
programme.desc: In a quest to attain manliness, Dipper ventures into the forest to battle the notorious Multi-Bear. Mabel tries to teach Grunkle Stan how to be attractive to women, particularly the waitress in the local diner, Lazy Susan.

programme.title: Gravity Falls: The Time Traveler's Pig
programme.desc: When Dipper wishes he could go back in time and undo a mistake he made, he discovers there is a time machine that can help him do just that. Meanwhile, it's love at first sight for Mabel when she wins a pet pig at the fair.

programme.title: Gravity Falls: Irrational Treasure
programme.desc: When Dipper and Mabel discover evidence that the reported town founder is a hoax, they set out to expose the historical coverup and prove Mabel's silliness is not a bad thing.

programme.title: Gravity Falls: Double Dipper
programme.desc: When Stan throws a big weekend party to promote the Mystery Shack, Dipper uses an off-kilter copy machine to make clones of himself so he can impress Wendy. Meanwhile, Mabel stands up for her friends and fights for the Party Queen title in their honour.

programme.title: Gravity Falls: Fight Fighters
programme.desc: After being challenged to a video game battle by Wendy's boyfriend Robbie, Dipper is shocked when his favourite playable fighter, Rumble McSkirmish, comes to life to defend him. Dipper soon loses control over Rumble and has to find a way to stop him.

programme.title: Gravity Falls: Little Dipper
programme.desc: Dipper feels self-conscious that Mabel is taller than him and is becoming the alpha twin, so he seeks out a magic way to grow himself the same height. Lil' Gideon takes advantage of Dipper's and Mabel's height discrepancies.

programme.title: Gravity Falls: The Deep End
programme.desc: When Mabel befriends a cute Merman trapped in the deep end of the public pool, she's determined to return him to his family in the ocean, even though that means her whirlwind romance will come to an end.

programme.title: Gravity Falls: Boss Mabel
programme.desc: When Mabel questions the way Sam runs the Shack, he challenges her to a bet: whoever can make the most money in three days wins the chance to run the Shack however they wish for the rest of the summer.

programme.title: Gravity Falls: Bottomless Pit!
programme.desc: When Stan, Dipper, Mabel and Soos find themselves falling into a bottomless pit, they each decide to tell a different story to pass the time.

programme.title: NFL Generations: Charm City
programme.desc: This latest installment is a charm city special episode, as a flock of Baltimore Ravens break down their favorite plays with their high-flying counterparts. Calais Campbell shares his love for shutdown corners, Mark Ingram dances and more.

programme.title: NFL GameDay View
programme.desc: The NFL Network's panel of experts pick every game on the schedule with insights from reporters around the country and in-depth statistical analysis. Plus, feedback from fans across the country as they make their vote on who will win each week.

programme.title: Saturdays in the South: A History of SEC Football: Part Three: 1960-1970
programme.desc: In the 1960s, Georgia Tech leaves the conference and the civil rights movement becomes embedded in the history and image of the region. Bear Bryant, hired in 1958, becomes the face of Alabama, and of SEC football.

programme.title: Saturdays in the South: A History of SEC Football: Part Four: 1969-1979
programme.desc: As a new decade dawns, quarterbacks Archie Manning, Bert Jones and Condredge Holloway build memorable legacies. Bear Bryant begins a new run of dominance at Alabama, and the rise of television sports transforms the SEC.

programme.title: Saturdays in the South: A History of SEC Football: Part Five: 1980-1989
programme.desc: The 1980s begin with Herschel Walker leading Georgia to a national title as a freshman. After Bear Bryant retires in 1982 and passes away four weeks later, control of the SEC is eventually transferred to Pat Dye and Bo Jackson at Auburn.

programme.title: Saturdays in the South: A History of SEC Football: Part Six: 1990-1999
programme.desc: The 1990s bring change with expansion and the sport's first-ever conference championship game. Florida's Steve Spurrier fashions an era of high-octane offense, and his Gators form a rivalry with Tennessee and quarterback Peyton Manning.

programme.title: Saturdays in the South: A History of SEC Football: Part Seven: 2000-2009
programme.desc: Nick Saban comes to LSU in 2000 and eventually wins the Tigers' first national championship since 1958 - a feat matched by Les Miles. At Florida, Tim Tebow becomes a cult hero and wins two national titles with Urban Meyer's Gators.

programme.title: Saturdays in the South: A History of SEC Football: Part Eight: 2010-2019
programme.desc: The SEC has a run of seven straight national titles, and nine in 12 years. Expansion in 2012 gives the conference 14 schools. Meanwhile, Nick Saban arguably becomes the greatest coach in the history of college football.

programme.title: Football Classics
programme.desc: November 10, 2012

programme.title: Football Classics
programme.desc: September 27, 2008

programme.title: Football Classics
programme.desc: October 12, 2013

programme.title: TrueSouth: Birmingham
programme.desc: Greek natives and their kin built the Birmingham restaurant scene, from barbecue joints to hotdog stands to steakhouses. Bright Star, founded in 1907, is the old guard and Johnny's is the new guard Greek restaurant. This is what the future looks like.

programme.title: TrueSouth: Athens
programme.desc: Pablo Rivadeneyra rotisserie-cooks lime-marinated chicken in Norwood. Across the street at Scott's BBQ, Larry and Betty Jean Scott work a smoke-belching pit. In these two places, we get a glimpse of the hard work that defines life in the south.

programme.title: TrueSouth: Nashville
programme.desc: One meat and three vegetables is the formula for a typical farmhand meal, translated in the mid-twentieth century to steam table cafes. In Nashville, the differences between Black and White cooking come into focus, and we discover that there are few.

programme.title: TrueSouth: Shreveport
programme.desc: Shreveport emerged in the mid-1800s as a lumber boomtown on the Red River. Cycles of boom and bust has defined this town. Herby K's is a 1936 vintage beer garden and fry house. Lucky Palace serves wok-fried T-bones and pork egg foo young.

programme.title: TrueSouth: New Orleans
programme.desc: From Liuzza's and Mandina's – two Sicilian corner stores that have become neighborhood Creole Italian restaurants – look at how and why some stories get erased while others become defining narratives.

programme.title: TrueSouth: Beaumont
programme.desc: Once a Southern institution, barbecue has become an American obsession and Texas brisket has been mainstreamed. This is a story about a scrappy onetime oil boomtown, where cooks make the most of scraps, and about two Gulf Coast dishes that have hung on.

programme.title: TrueSouth: Hodgenville
programme.desc: In the southern towns of Hodgenville and Bardstown, Kentucky, the land where Lincoln was born, conflicting ideas about identity and purpose come into focus. Onion burgers and country sausage prove constants in a time of great change.

programme.title: TrueSouth: Memphis
programme.desc: Just as there are many Souths, black Memphis reveals itself to be varied and complex and beautiful. Here, we see the streets of Memphis and its classic spots including Pop's Hot Tamales, Lee Crumb and Ms. Girlee's.

programme.title: The Story Trek: Excited About Nothing
programme.desc: Todd gets sent to one of the Rocky Mountain states, where he knocks on doors until someone agrees to be on TV. Todd then braves single-digit temperatures to find amazing human drama and even his own private rap concert.

programme.title: Welcome Home: Back on Track

programme.title: Movie: Shrek Forever After

programme.title: Movie: The Jungle Book
programme.desc: An orphan boy raised in the jungle embarks on an epic adventure to return to civilization with the help of a stern panther and an affable bear.

programme.title: NCIS: New Orleans: The Recruits

programme.title: NCIS: New Orleans: Stolen Valor

programme.title: Forensic Files: Van-ished
programme.desc: A twelve-year-old girl claimed she had been abducted and sexually assaulted. She recounted what happened in such a flat, unemotional voice that police found it difficult to believe her.

programme.title: Forensic Files: A Novel Idea
programme.desc: When a frantic call from a man claiming his wife fell down the stairs brings investigators to their home, they are concerned by the unusually large number of bloodstains, and how big they are.

programme.title: Forensic Files: Army of Evidence
programme.desc: A mother of two children is found dead near suicide notes and a pistol. At first it is assumed to be a suicide, but when police learn she had nearly been killed in a fire three years earlier, new suspicions are raised.

programme.title: Forensic Files: Shear Luck
programme.desc: Investigators have to reassemble a computer disk that has been cut to pieces with shears, in order to determine the guilt or innocence of an Air Force serviceman suspected of brutally murdering his wife.

programme.title: Forensic Files: Shell Game
programme.desc: A tiny clue, no bigger than a thumbtack, held all the information investigators needed to put a trio of cold-blooded killers behind bars.

programme.title: Forensic Files: Ring Him Up
programme.desc: For ten years, the disappearance of a college co-ed was a mystery until new scientific testing cast a different light on a man who had been a suspect all along.

programme.title: Forensic Files: Cereal Killer
programme.desc: Police must search for a boy who goes missing mysteriously after his home burns down and are disheartened when they find his body, but the killer left behind numerous clues.

programme.title: Forensic Files: Crash Course
programme.desc: A highway patrolman is shocked by a coroner's ruling of an 'accidental death.' after he attends a Crash scene with far more blood than usual. An anonymous tip reinvigorates the investigation.

programme.title: Forensic Files: Undertaken
programme.desc: The medical examiner ruled the death an accident, but the detectives investigating the case thought the evidence at the scene indicated otherwise. It would take three years, an exhumation and a second autopsy to determine who was right.

programme.title: Forensic Files: Dark Waters
programme.desc: Investigators face the daunting task of searching for one boat among many others after a hit-and-run boating accident caused the death of a young man. Detectives hope witnesses to the accident can help them.

programme.title: Forensic Files: As the Tide Turns
programme.desc: A woman is found dead. A man's version of events was at variance with the evidence, so investigators turned to forensic science, hoping to determine if this was a botched robbery, or cold-blooded murder.

programme.title: Forensic Files: A Tight Leash
programme.desc: Law enforcement officials turn to forensic scientists when they experience difficulty in their attempts to figure out how a stalker was able to steal his victim's personal photographs and obtain the code to her home security system.

programme.title: Forensic Files: Wired for Disaster
programme.desc: A 29-year-old woman is killed instantly when a bomb explodes in her home. The bomber not only had knowledge and skill, but also a motive for murder.

programme.title: Forensic Files: Wood-Be Killer
programme.desc: A murderer thought he could cover his tracks by setting fire to the crime scene, but in doing so, he provided the forensic evidence needed to convict him; a new forensic tool used to examine the kindling proved to be his undoing.

programme.title: Forensic Files: Murder, She Wrote
programme.desc: A woman suffering from depression apparently took her own life. But her sister told police, that a year before her death, she said if anything were to happen to her, there was a note in the china cabinet. Investigators found the note and the killer.

programme.title: Forensic Files: Concrete Alibi

programme.title: Forensic Files: Internal Affair
programme.desc: Friends and family of a woman gone missing were determined to find her until their worst fears were confirmed weeks later. Blood evidence and computer forensics helped investigators to catch the killer, and convince the jury of his guilt.

programme.title: Forensic Files: A Case of the Flue
programme.desc: In 1993, when an 83 year old woman was found dead in her home, investigators used clues like flecks of paint, t-shirt fibres, and a cellular phone to work out just how, and why, she died.

programme.title: Forensic Files: Up in Smoke
programme.desc: When an elderly couple die in a housefire their son becomes the prime suspect. The son insists that he is innocent, and that his efforts to extinguish the fire made it worse. Investigators turn to science to determine the truth.

programme.title: Forensic Files: Soiled Plan
programme.desc: Police instituted an intense search after a mother of two young children went missing. Days later they found her body. Then the case went cold for twenty years, until advances in technology allowed the investigators to see the case in a new light.

programme.title: Deadliest Catch: Bering Sea Wrecking Ball
programme.desc: Stuffed pots come at a price as Jake scrambles to repair a slack tank before it kills his crab or capsizes the Saga. Josh and Casey's rudder fails in heavy traffic and Wild Bill's new greenhorn makes an easy target for an out-of-control picking hook.

programme.title: Deadliest Catch: Danger Close
programme.desc: Josh and Casey race to haul pots as engine failure threatens their delivery window. As seawater destroys his hydraulic motors, Junior attempts a risky high seas delivery of three new motors. Harley and his crew bang heads over his fishing strategy.

programme.title: Deadliest Catch: Like Father Like Daughter
programme.desc: Sig and Mandy conspire to get Jake to do their prospecting, but the former deckhand seizes the moment to fill his own tanks. The Wizard crew must break through ice to rush Keith to a doctor. Junior fears the worst as a deckhand falls from the stack.

programme.title: Deadliest Catch: On Deck: Supermoon Snowstorm
programme.desc: A cyclonic snowstorm churns up rogue waves, Jake risks capsizing and Monte's move pays off in big pots. As the storm peaks, the Cornelia Marie's deck turns greenhorn gauntlet, fog overtakes the fleet, Harley loses an engine and Junior fights sea-fleas.

programme.title: Deadliest Catch: End of the Beginning
programme.desc: As the fleet mounts a final Winter season push, sad news rattles the Cornelia Marie wheelhouse. Lady Alaska greenhorn, Gabby, overcomes a painful injury to support her crew. Wild Bill's deckhands must devise a bush fix or face hauling pots by hand.

programme.title: Deadliest Catch: Blood is Thicker Than Water
programme.desc: Saga loses a veteran deckhand, forcing Jake to steam a greenhorn army to the Russian line. Amid scuttlebutt that ports are closing due to a virus, Josh fights to get home for a reunion with his brother and Sig's daughter gets rushed to the hospital.

programme.title: Deadliest Catch: Everything Changes
programme.desc: The Cornelia crew fights to save their gear from sea ice. Jake risks the far north to fill his tanks before his son's birth. Amid news of a looming pandemic, Russian boats mysteriously disappear and captains race to dock before crab prices crash.

programme.title: Josh Gates Tonight: Full Stream Ahead
programme.desc: Josh Gates explores Discovery's brand-new streaming platform, discovery+, with some of its biggest stars including David Schwimmer, Mike Rowe, Faruq Tauheed of Battlebots as well as Freddy Dodge and Juan Ibarra from Gold Rush.

programme.title: 100 Days Wild: And Then There Were None
programme.desc: The battle over the food supply becomes the last straw for Adam, Evan, Christine and Gerrid. Andrew and Jennifer are abandoned at base camp. Adam and Evan join Oliver on a nearby claim, leading to a final confrontation with Andrew.

programme.title: Tomorrow's World Today: The Gift of Life
programme.desc: Lifesaving medical technology takes Darieth to San Francisco to explore the world of transplant care. Transplant has evolved with DNA sequencing and Artificial Intelligence to give patients the best care before and after transplantation.

programme.title: The Elite Fishing League: Championship Round 6 Tidewater Tour Champ
programme.desc: The five round winners compete for the title of Tidewater Tour Champion.

programme.title: Island Hoping Florida Keys

programme.title: Nations United: Urgent Solutions for Urgent Times
programme.desc: In the midst of a global pandemic a focus on facts and solutions is important.

programme.title: Josh Gates Tonight: Full Stream Ahead
programme.desc: Josh Gates explores Discovery's brand-new streaming platform, discovery+, with some of its biggest stars including David Schwimmer, Mike Rowe, Faruq Tauheed of Battlebots as well as Freddy Dodge and Juan Ibarra from Gold Rush.

programme.title: Gold Rush: It's Alive
programme.desc: Rick bets nearly all his gold on a new machine that could transform his operation. Tony resurrects an abandoned secret weapon. Parker hires new crew members to make up for lost time.

programme.title: Gold Rush: Big Red vs. Sluicifer
programme.desc: A competition erupts between Parker's two wash plant teams. Tony discovers a pile of lost gold. Fred Lewis' and veteran crew fire up The Colonel and face an unexpected setback.

programme.title: Gold Rush: Rick's Sixth Sense
programme.desc: Rick risks his season on a hunch that he's discovered new hot spot. Tony mobilizes an arsenal to cash in on lost gold. Fred's veteran crew fight old equipment.

programme.title: Gold Rush: Lost Gold of Paradise Hill
programme.desc: Tony fires up a second plant to haul in gold left behind by the old-timers. Tensions rise when Parker departs for Alaska, leaving Mitch in charge. Rick faces disaster when he his sluice box springs a leak.

programme.title: Gold Rush: Back to Alaska
programme.desc: Parker returns to Alaska to prospect new ground and expand his empire. Rick digs deep for big gold in a cut he hopes will deliver the biggest weigh in of the season. Fred's crew member Johnny comes up with unique fix to their water problem.

programme.title: Alaska: The Last Frontier: We're Marooned
programme.desc: While transporting calves to the Head of the Bay by boat, Otto, Charlotte and August are marooned. Still struggling with PTSD, Atz Lee works with his son Etienne to finish the insulation on his backcountry build and Jewel visits the homestead.

programme.title: Untold Stories of the ER: Shocking!
programme.desc: A pianist's finger is accidentally sawed off and missing until his wife and dog come to the ER. A man's pacemaker is killing him, yet keeping him alive, and after his bachelor party, a nurse's fiancé is behaving bizarrely.

programme.title: Untold Stories of the ER: Clueless!
programme.desc: When a man believes he is constipated, he visits the ER only to find out his condition is much worse. A young woman experiences cardiac arrest and the doctor finds she's treating two patients in one. Also, a patient's family is entirely clueless.

programme.title: 1000-lb Sisters: Meet the Slaton Sisters
programme.desc: The Slaton's are larger-than-life sisters who have always relied on each other and food for support. Amy, age 31, is desperate to have children, but hasn't been able to due to her weight. While Tammy, age 32, is one step away from being bedridden.

programme.title: 1000-lb Sisters: 1,000 Pounds to Freedom
programme.desc: Amy and Tammy go to Atlanta to meet Dr. Procter to discuss weight loss surgery. When they arrive, Dr. Procter gives Tammy a hefty weight loss goal before he will approve her for surgery, meanwhile the Dietician has more bad news for Amy.

programme.title: 1000-lb Sisters: Breaking Point
programme.desc: Amy and Tammy eagerly begin their weight loss journey. Sticking to their diets is a matter of life or death but proves to be a real struggle. The pressure to reach their goals culminates in a huge fight that threatens their diets and relationship.

programme.title: 1000-lb Sisters: A House Divided
programme.desc: After a stressful first month of dieting, the sisters head back to Atlanta for their one-month check in with Dr. Procter to find out if they are on the right path to their weight goals. The results force Amy to make a difficult decision.

programme.title: 1000-lb Sisters: Wedding and Weigh-ins
programme.desc: Amy decides to have a real wedding ceremony because if she gets approved for surgery, she's afraid she may not survive. After the wedding, the sisters head to Atlanta hoping for a life-changing final weigh-in.

programme.title: 1000-lb Sisters: Under the Knife
programme.desc: Amy undergoes gastric bypass surgery to change her life, but endangers herself by not sticking to the post-op diet. Tammy seeks support from her love interest, Jerry. Two months later, the sisters reveal the changes they've made to family and friends.

programme.title: 1000-lb Sisters: Breaking Point
programme.desc: Amy and Tammy eagerly begin their weight loss journey. Sticking to their diets is a matter of life or death but proves to be a real struggle. The pressure to reach their goals culminates in a huge fight that threatens their diets and relationship.

programme.title: Say Yes to the Dress: Atlanta: A Case of Tunnel Vision

programme.title: Shark Tank
programme.desc: A confident young entrepreneur impresses the sharks with a lucrative line of trendy celebrity fashion accessories. Also, Kevin O'Leary shows how much he hates to lose when he follows entrepreneurs out of the shark tank to have the last word.

programme.title: Shark Tank
programme.desc: Budding entrepreneurs are given the chance to make their business dreams come true and become successful or even wealthy. But the entrepreneurs must first try to convince five tough, multi-millionaire tycoons to fund their ideas.

programme.title: SpongeBob SquarePants: It Came From Goo Lagoon

programme.title: SpongeBob SquarePants: Escape from Beneath Glove World

programme.title: The Casagrandes: Cursed!

programme.desc: Captain Man and Schwoz have a new group of four young superheroes to train.

programme.title: Young Sheldon: A Loaf of Bread and a Grand Old Flag

programme.title: Young Sheldon: Albert Einstein and the Story of Another Mary
programme.desc: Inspired by his hero, Albert Einstein, Sheldon tries his hand at the violin. Also, Mary and Meemaw secretly buy a pregnancy test when Mary thinks she's pregnant.

programme.title: Friends: The One After the Super Bowl
programme.desc: Ross decides to track down Marcel; Phoebe plays songs for children at the library.

programme.title: Friends: The One After the Super Bowl
programme.desc: Chandler is left in a restaurant wearing only underwear; Rachel and Monica have a fight.

programme.title: Friends: The One With the Prom Video

programme.title: Friends: The One Where Ross and Rachel ...You Know
programme.desc: Monica is attracted to an old family friend. Ross and Rachel have their first date.

programme.title: Friends: The One Where Joey Moves Out
programme.desc: Phoebe talks Rachel into getting a tattoo with her and then chickens out; Joey moves out.

programme.title: Friends: The One Where Eddie Moves In

programme.title: Mom: Snickerdoodle and a Nip Slip

programme.title: Mom: Quaaludes and Crackerjack
programme.desc: In order to avoid her ex-boyfriend, Bonnie drags Christy to a new AA meeting, only to run into her ex-girlfriend, Jeanine, from 30 years ago.

programme.title: Full House: Nerd for a Day

programme.title: Full House: Star Search

programme.title: Full House: And They Call It Puppy Love

programme.title: Full House: Dr. Dare Rides Again

programme.title: SpongeBob SquarePants: Krusty Koncessionaires/ Dream Hoppers

programme.title: The Loud House: Predict Ability / Driving Ambition
programme.desc: Lincoln fears he's predictable, so vows to change up his routine. Then, Lori gets the yips when she learns the golf coach from her dream college is coming to see her play.

programme.title: The Loud House: Really Loud Music
programme.desc: Luna contemplates whether she should go with a song that's true to her sound, or try to make something that the whole world will love just to win a song writing contest.

programme.title: The Loud House: Back Out There/ Spell It Out
programme.desc: Clyde, worried that Lincoln is hung up on Ronnie Anne, tries to cure his post-breakup blues.Tired of her siblings walking all over her, Lucy turns to a spell book once to teach her siblings a lesson.

programme.title: The Loud House: Making the Grade/ Vantastic Voyage
programme.desc: When Lincoln's four-year-old sister, Lisa, gets put into his 5th grade class, his social standing is at stake. Sick of their family van always falling apart, the kids decide to try and trick Dad into buying a cool new van.

programme.title: The Casagrandes: What's Love Gato Do With It/ Dial M. for Mustard
programme.desc: After a magician hypnotizes Bobby into thinking he's a cat, the family needs to snap him out of it. Ronnie Anne and friends try to uncover what happened to Bruno's hot dog cart.

programme.title: The Loud House: Tripped!

programme.title: The Loud House: The Write Stuff/ Racing Hearts
programme.desc: When Lucy quits the writing club at school due to the by-the-books way Huggins runs the club, Mom takes over. When Luna and Sam embark on a challenge taking place across Royal Woods, they discover they have less in common than they thought.

programme.title: The Loud House: Love Birds/ Rocket Men
programme.desc: Lincoln and Clyde beg their parents to let them go to space cadets camp, but it's not as fun as they expected. After Walt's failed attempt at romance, Charles, Cliff and Geo try to find their pal a love connection.

programme.title: The Loud House: Kings of the Con
programme.desc: The girls get more attention when accompany Lincoln and Clyde to the Ace Savvy convention dressed as the Full deck.

programme.title: The Loud House: Ghosted!
programme.desc: Lori gets help from Lincoln and Clyde to banish a ghost that's haunting her, but Fairway's golf team starts struggling so she has to bring it back.

programme.title: The Casagrandes: Cursed!
programme.desc: Celebrity psychic Ernesto Estrella deems Great Lakes City to be bad luck and Abuela believes him.

programme.title: The Loud House: Wheel and Deal/ Feast or Family
programme.desc: Lana wants to win a race car ride-along with her hero, so she and Lincoln enter to compete in a soap box derby.When Luan must enroll in a culinary class, Dad uses it to share his passion for cooking with one of his kids.

programme.title: The Loud House: Snoop's On/ How Double Dare You
programme.desc: The siblings turn to Lisa's genius to help them land a spot on 'Double Dare.' After secretly reading Luna's diary, Leni and Lincoln become worried about her.

programme.title: The Loud House: The Loudest Mission: Relative Chaos
programme.desc: Ronnie Anne and Bobby visit their crazy, chaotic extended family. Bobby fits in great but Ronnie Anne struggles with the smothering nature of their relatives. Little do they know, their mother has a secret motive.

programme.title: So You Think You'd Survive?: Tornados and Killer Bees
programme.desc: What do you do when a tornado is about to strike? Could you survive an attack of killer bees? Incredible caught-on-tape moments challenge you with live power lines, dangerous sinkholes, enormous blizzards, massive landslides and vicious sunburns!

programme.title: So You Think You'd Survive?: Frostbite and Bears
programme.desc: Looking at how to survive pursuit by a wild bear and being trapped in a sinking car. Real-life footage tests your instincts against wildfires, frostbite, extreme fog and treacherous underwater currents.

programme.title: So You Think You'd Survive?: Floods and Spiders
programme.desc: Test your knowledge of poisonous spiders, big wave wipeouts, icy hills and tumbleweeds on the attack! Find out if you'd know what to do if you were trapped in a flash flood or small plane crash.

programme.title: World's Deadliest Weather: Escaping Catastrophe
programme.desc: A deadly volcano eruption in Guatemala unleashes a searing hot mass of ash, lava and toxic fumes, leaving firefighters trapped in their vehicle and fighting for survival.

programme.title: World's Deadliest Weather: Nature's Fury
programme.desc: Hurricane Florence brings biblical flooding to North Carolina, trapping a mother and son in their car and prompting the US Coastguard to carry out vital search and rescue missions.

programme.title: Mayday: Blind Landing
programme.desc: Investigators face a major challenge when looters make off with key evidence from the crash of TANS Peru Flight 204. It takes all their ingenuity to reconstruct the final moments of the flight to find out why the 737 crashed in the jungle.

programme.title: Heavy Rescue: 401: Not Your Regular Fender Bender
programme.desc: A runaway van smashes through a wall and into the path of a full-speed truck. The crew hurries to save a load of pharmaceuticals before it freezes.

programme.title: Fat Guys in the Woods: Man Down, So Man Up
programme.desc: An emergency hits the woods around the Smoky Mountains when 47-year old Johnny Frisbee leaves the episode half way through with an irregular heartbeat.

programme.title: SOS: How to Survive: Trapped in a Blizzard
programme.desc: The Kim family take a wrong turn and find themselves stuck in snow on a logging road. With no knowledge about how survive the cold and mountains the Kim's get desperate. After 3 days they run out of food, gas and hopes of another car coming to save them.

programme.title: SOS: How to Survive: Deadly Cold
programme.desc: Rulon Gardner won the Greco-Roman wrestling gold medal in the 2000 Olympics. Two years later, he got soaked when his snowmobile was stuck in a river. With temperatures plunging to 25 below zero, he faced his toughest opponent: death.

programme.title: SOS: How to Survive: Delirious in the Desert
programme.desc: Just a few hours into bouldering in Joshua Tree Park, Paul Hanks slips and falls about 20 feet, shattering his left heel and fracturing his pelvis. Disoriented and unable to walk, he falls a second time and fractures his skull.

programme.title: Top Ten Extreme Jobs
programme.desc: Extreme jobs are not for everyone, but if you're willing to battle raging weather and risk life and limb for your paycheck, you may be a perfect candidate. We are counting down the top ten most extreme jobs on the planet.

programme.title: Top Ten Weather Phobias
programme.desc: Weather can be shocking and terrifying, but if a healthy respect of Mother Nature turns into an irrational fear, you may have a weather phobia. You aren't alone, as one in ten people have weather phobias, and we count down the top ten in this special.

programme.title: Top Ten Weather Disasters
programme.desc: Its man and machine versus weather, and all too often we lose the fight. Engineering catastrophies, mechanical mishaps and human missteps, with one common catalyst, the weather. We count down the top ten weather disasters that made history.

programme.desc: An amnesiac is rescued at sea by the crew of an Italian fishing boat. Nearly dead, he carries nothing but the bullets in his back and the bank account number embedded in his hip. He must try to piece together his mysterious past while eluding assassins.

programme.title: Movie: The Bourne Supremacy
programme.desc: A former CIA assassin from an experimental top-secret program resorts to his training when he is framed for a botched CIA operation.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Inside Jokes: The Party Crasher
programme.desc: Find out which jokers were in on the surprise Gary Busey appearance, where the phrase 'tit for tat' came from, and how Murr's big mouth backfired on him, leading to this Statue of Liberty punishment.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Inside Jokes: Make Womb for Daddy
programme.desc: Find out who scored free sandwiches while shooting at Blimpie; what classic game inspired the 'Guess What' challenge; and what was worse for Q than the pain he endured during his 'childbirth' punishment.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Inside Jokes: Snow Way Out
programme.desc: Find out which Joker only drinks coffee after 2pm; who is most self-conscious about singing; and how long did Joe and Q wait inside while Murr and Sal hung out on a ski lift in the freezing cold.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Inside Jokes: Sneaking Number Twos, Going Number One
programme.desc: Find out why the jokers love filming at the grocery store, how many pencils were purchased for put-pocketing and why Sal's pee punishment was twice as terrible as it looked.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Inside Jokes: Car Sick
programme.desc: Find out what celebrity Sal wrote fan letters to as a kid, which grocery game is Murr's favourite, how the guys found sloppy Joe and who helped Q's first jeep punishment a milky mess.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Inside Jokes: Dover and Out
programme.desc: The guys survey employees with odd questions, and tonight's loser unveils a mural in a small town whose residents react with big city rage.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Inside Jokes: The Marathon Man
programme.desc: Find out which joker has pet parakeets, why the crew had to work in a truck down the block while filming at a bagel shop and who got to sound the start horn for Murr's race across the Brooklyn Bridge.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Inside Jokes: Guilty as Charged
programme.desc: Find out why Q messed with a fishmonger at Fairway instead of a shopper, what nearly caused Sal to lose it during his UFO presentation and how the crew nabbed close ups from inside the mob during Q's punishment.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Like a Boss
programme.desc: The jokers act fast and furiously as employees at a theme park and go postal on model citizens who don't realize they're part of the picture. Then, tonight's big loser tumbles and falls after antagonizing a room full of Guy Fieri fans.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Dropping Knowledge
programme.desc: The guys pump the breaks on a bike ride to ask perplexed strangers for help and cut the java line to get the go-ahead on some very strange emails. Plus, tonight's big loser gets schooled in a game show that barely makes the grade.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Hump Day
programme.desc: The guys geek out while lending a hand to customers at a casual restaurant, then battle across the table of a focus group. Plus, tonight's big loser gets spitting mad when forced to spend some quality time at the zoo.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Autograph Corrector
programme.desc: The guys rely on the kindness of strangers to help answer some awkward questions, then go looking for love in the park. Plus, tonight's big loser strikes out amongst baseball fans with an assist from Yankees legend Tino Martinez.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: The Bogey Man
programme.desc: The laughing gas flows freely when the guys pose as dental assistants. Later, they make headlines for all the wrong reasons, and get burned giving some campfire tips. Plus, tonight’s big loser scores a hole in none on the links.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Hell on Wheels
programme.desc: The guys try and work out the kinks as physical therapists, then pitch some way out-of-the-box ideas for TV hosts. Plus, the big loser gets rolled over by some speedy ladies.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Crash Test Dummies
programme.desc: Murr, Sal, Joe and Q take some customers for a wild ride, and things get messy when they ask fellow shoppers for a hand. Plus, the big losers suffer a monster punishment that leaves them crushed.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: The Closer
programme.desc: Q, Joe, Sal and Murr get tech help from some confused tutors, then play hot potato in a shoe store. Plus, the big loser wishes he could press 'escape' during a brutal coffee shop punishment.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Full Mental Jacket
programme.desc: Joe, Murr, Q and Sal send blood pressure rates sky-high while posing as nurses, then stroll through the store with their wives. Plus, the big loser wishes he could check out early at a pop-up fashion show.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Blue Man Dupe
programme.desc: Murr, Joe, Q and Sal are a little too generous with fellow shoppers at the market, then ask strangers to help them out of a texting jam. Plus, the big loser fights his way, repeatedly, to the best seat in the house.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Well...
programme.desc: Q, Sal, Joe and Murr play a game of tag at a coffee shop, then convince shoppers to deliver sketchy messages at the grocery store. Plus, tonight's big loser might lose his mind in Impractical Jokers' first-ever non-punishment punishment.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: The Prize Fighter
programme.desc: The guys get their checks bounced by customers who notice unusual charges, then help out coworkers with some peculiar trust exercises. Later, the big loser finds himself deep at a carnival game that's rigged for him to lose.

programme.title: Top Secret Videos: Fitness Goals
programme.desc: Our interns examine surveillance videos, including a crocodile that breaks up a swimming pool hook-up and a bruising yacht dive fail.

programme.title: Top Secret Videos: Operation Hidden Roof
programme.desc: Featured in this episode are a machete-wielding vigilante in underwear, a swimming pool selfie stick debacle and a hoofed menace. Plus, the NSA interns dispose of an oddly erotic project.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Inside Jokes: Stripteased
programme.desc: Find out what prop Q asked to keep from the dental damn challenge, what glazed delight is known to give Joe great power and how a doctor's appointment inspired Murr's striptease punishment.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Inside Jokes: Turning the Tables
programme.desc: Find out how the Jokers keep their cover while filming at the mall, and Q's favorite VR word. Plus, learn how long Joe was sore after the table-busting punishment.

programme.title: The Carbonaro Effect: Double Takes: Back to the Big Dig
programme.desc: Michael jumps back in time and unleashes unseen content for a fresh spin on classic Carbonaro bits. Michael reboots a living skeleton, an instant pet trap, a flipping mermaid and more, all with brand-new reactions.

programme.title: The Carbonaro Effect: Double Takes: Back to Back in Time
programme.desc: Michael jumps back in time and unleashes unseen content for a fresh spin on classic Carbonaro bits. Michael revisits a spirits-drinking spirit, a living fossil, rewrites history again and more with brand-new reactions.

programme.title: The Carbonaro Effect
programme.desc: Michael Carbonaro performs magic tricks on unsuspecting victims, all caught on camera.

programme.title: The Carbonaro Effect: Double Takes: Back to Fight or Flight
programme.desc: Michael jumps back in time and unleashes unseen content for a fresh spin on classic Carbonaro bits. Michael reanimates a chameleon fish, reboots lengthy corks, remakes the airport bird man, and more with all-new reactions.

programme.title: The Carbonaro Effect: Lipstick and the Flying Nun
programme.desc: While working at an antique shop, Michael unpacks an item with a message from beyond. Plus, he works a fundraiser where a painting raises not only money but the body of a nun.

programme.title: The Carbonaro Effect: Chilly Flakes and Wormholes
programme.desc: Michael works in a spice shop and gets an icy treatment with his take on 'chilly flakes'. Plus, he cleans a spill at a hotel with his phone. Afterwards, at an abandoned house, he amazes his assistant by climbing through a wormhole.

programme.title: The Carbonaro Effect: Cat Lady
programme.desc: At auditions, Michael wows directors with costume changes but cracks when he sings. Plus, things get sticky when he demos flying post-its. Then, Michael's co-worker's jaw drops when a shape-shifting tenant returns while they clean house.

programme.title: The Carbonaro Effect: Puppification
programme.desc: Michael blends juices with a sticker at an appliance store, and then reverses the food chain ay a pet shop. Later, at a doggy spa, a rejuvenation machine turns back time.

programme.title: TruTV's Top Funniest: Side Splitters
programme.desc: The most side-splitting fails, gags, and flubs including two kids who cover their house in flour, a high school science experience blows everyone away, and the world's worst pet keeper.

programme.title: TruTV's Top Funniest: Hilarious Moments
programme.desc: The greatest mistakes and mishaps ever caught on tape including an angry video gamer who goes from high score down to the floor, a young woman who takes her first swing at a golf ball, and a magician who turns a romantic dream into a reality.

programme.title: TruTV's Top Funniest: Ridiculous Antics
programme.desc: TruTV’s Top Funniest splits your sides with the most hilarious videos from across the globe. Watch as a baby elephant gets frisky with the visitors at the zoo, and see what happens when a dad decides to jump into a frozen pool.

programme.title: TruTV's Top Funniest: Moments of Regret
programme.desc: Watch celebrity look-a-likes run amok on an unsuspecting public. Plus, a painful tribute to those moments that may you say 'ow!'

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Ash Clown
programme.desc: The guys give customers an eye opening experience at a glasses store, and convince customers to pass illicit, and ridiculous, messages while shopping for groceres.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Stage Fright
programme.desc: The guys serve up raw deals at a pawn shop counter and try to convince strangers to give them hugs. Plus, tonight's loser performs his life story through song.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Lady and the Tramp
programme.desc: The guys us a two-way mirror to get each other to crack, and tonight's loser gets taken for a ride that ends at the Statue of Liberty.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: The Parent Trap

programme.desc: The guys compete in their first four-way challenge. Later, see what happens when they try to lead a kid's art class, and which loser fails so badly that he's hit with an on-the-spot punishment that leaves him seeing red.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Medium, Well Done
programme.desc: Murr, Sal, Q, and Joe pose as TV execs pitching insane shows, and tonight's big loser undergoes a psychic reading that puts his real life on the wrong track.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Dover and Out
programme.desc: The guys survey employees with odd questions, and tonight's loser unveils a mural in a small town whose residents react with big city rage.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Out of Left Field
programme.desc: The guys try not to crack up during a four-way face off and later find that a stroll with the Gatto pups is no walk in the park. Plus, tonight's big loser suffers more than muscle failure in a major league embarrassment.

programme.desc: Carrie wonders why her guy won't hit the sheets; Miranda discovers she has an STD.

programme.title: Sex and the City: Drama Queens
programme.desc: Carrie is nervous about meeting Aidan's family; Charlotte meets a doctor.

programme.desc: Carrie bumps into Big at a party; Samantha fears menopause; Miranda breaks up with Steve.

programme.title: Sex and the City: Easy Come, Easy Go

programme.desc: Carrie's fling weighs on her conscience; Charlotte plays hardball over a pre-nup.

programme.title: Sex and the City: Escape From New York
programme.desc: While visiting L.A., Samantha meets a model and Carrie meets Matthew McConaughey.

programme.title: Sex and the City: Sex and Another City
programme.desc: While in Los Angeles, the girls head to the Playboy Mansion for a pool party.

programme.desc: Charlotte tackles Trey's problem, while Samantha throws a party for a mature 13-year-old.

programme.desc: Miranda is shocked to learn that her date has died; Carrie teaches a seminar.

programme.desc: When Big invites Carrie to lunch, she ignores Miranda's advice and accepts his invitation.

programme.title: Sex and the City: The Real Me

programme.title: Sex and the City: Defining Moments

programme.title: Sex and the City: What's Sex Got to Do With It?
programme.desc: Samantha actually begins to date someone that she likes; Miranda opts for celibacy.

programme.title: Sex and the City: Ghost Town

programme.title: Sex and the City: Baby, Talk Is Cheap
programme.desc: Carrie is still crazy about Aidan and wants him back; Charlotte rethinks her baby plans.

programme.title: Sex and the City: Time and Punishment
programme.desc: Carrie frets about Aidan's ability to forgive her affair; Miranda discovers leisure.

programme.title: Sex and the City: My Motherboard, Myself
programme.desc: Carrie and Aidan exchange keys; Miranda strains to maintain her strong woman image.

programme.title: Sex and the City: Sex and the Country
programme.desc: Samantha joins Carrie at Aidan's cottage in the country where she meets a cute farmer.

programme.title: Sex and the City: Belles of the Balls
programme.desc: Carrie helps Aidan and Big learn to accept each other, while Miranda and Steve get closer.

programme.title: Sex and the City: Coulda, Woulda, Shoulda
programme.desc: Miranda reveals that she's pregnant, and after much deliberation, she decides to have the baby. Meanwhile, Samantha uses her connection as a representative for Lucy Liu to her advantage so she can nab a great handbag.

programme.title: Sex and the City: Just Say Yes
programme.desc: Carrie is nervous when Aidan suggests that they move in together. To make matters worse, she finds an engagement ring among his things and questions whether he is 'the one.' Miranda and Steve decide to raise the baby together, while living separately.

programme.title: Sex and the City: The Good Fight
programme.desc: Once Aidan moves in to her apartment, Carrie feels crowded. Meanwhile, Samantha is unnerved when she realizes she is falling in love with her boss. Elsewhere, Miranda is worried that having sex will hurt the baby.

programme.title: Sex and the City: All That Glitters
programme.desc: Carrie befriends a gay guy but she soon realizes that she has to fight for his attention when they go out together. While under the influence of ecstasy, Samantha confesses to Richard that she loves him. Miranda's co-worker tells everyone she is pregnant.

programme.title: Sex and the City: Change of a Dress
programme.desc: When Carrie tells Aidan she wants to take things slower, he refuses to give her time and moves out of their apartment the following day. Meanwhile, the monogamy bug bites Samantha when she finds out Richard is sleeping with other women.

programme.title: Sex and the City: Ring a Ding Ding
programme.desc: Carrie has a serious financial problem; Miranda and Steve grapple with raising a baby.

programme.title: Sex and the City: A Vogue Idea
programme.desc: Carrie begins her new job at Vogue; Charlotte hosts Miranda's baby shower.

programme.title: Sex and the City: I Heart NY
programme.desc: Carrie becomes nostalgic about her relationship with Mr. Big and the city she loves.

programme.title: Sex and the City: Anchors Away
programme.desc: The girls try to come to terms with Miranda's new life with Brady; Carrie meets a sailor.

programme.title: Sex and the City: Unoriginal Sin

programme.title: Sex and the City: Luck Be an Old Lady
programme.desc: The girls head to Atlantic City for a 'girls' weekend; Samantha can't trust Richard.

programme.title: Sex and the City: Cover Girl
programme.desc: Samantha offers to help Carrie with her book cover; Charlotte shops on-line.

programme.title: Sex and the City: Plus One Is the Loneliest Number
programme.desc: Samantha organizes Carrie's book-launch party; Miranda doesn't tell her date about Brady.

programme.title: Sex and the City: Critical Condition
programme.desc: Carrie obsesses over a comment made about Aidan; Samantha baby-sits Brady.

programme.title: Sex and the City: The Big Journey
programme.desc: Hoping to see Big, Carrie travels to a book reading in San Francisco with Samantha.

programme.title: Sex and the City: I Love a Charade
programme.desc: The girls head to the Hamptons for the memorable wedding of a supposedly gay singer.

programme.title: Sex and the City: To Market, to Market

programme.desc: Carrie is disappointed by Jack's lacklustre performance between the sheets.

programme.title: Sex and the City: Pick a Little, Talk a Little
programme.desc: Berger reacts poorly to Carrie's criticism; Charlotte and Harry have an argument.

programme.title: Chopped: Comfort Food Feud: Pizza
programme.desc: The $25,000 Comfort Food Feud continues with a battle devoted to pizza. The competitors find spaghetti and meatballs in an unusual form in the entree basket, and the dessert round involves some spicy, salty and sugary ingredients.

programme.title: Chopped: Comfort Food Feud: Finale
programme.desc: Four Comfort Food Feud champions return to a decked-out outdoor arena to battle for the $25,000 prize and the title of Chopped Grand Champion. In each round, the ingredient baskets contain delicious reminders of the tournament's culinary themes.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives: Holiday Hoopla
programme.desc: This trip, Guy Fieri's celebrating the holidays with dishes perfect for the Christmas and Hanukkah season. In California, he's hitting up joints cooking up savoury stuffing and lights-out latkes.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives: Cross Country Comfort Food
programme.desc: Guy is finding great food from the ski slopes to the sea. In Miami, a law student who has traded in his suit for a chef coat is making all kinds of comfort food, from spicy ox tail to big ragu.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives: Worldwide Flavorfest
programme.desc: Guy starts this trip in Cincinnati at a Jamaican joint firing up their trademark jerk chicken and spicy snapper. Then, he stops ion Boston at a lunch place that is serving up authentic Italian.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives: Sweet, Savory and Saucy
programme.desc: This trip, Guy's taste buds are in for a culinary adventure. In Bakersfield, Calif., the classic cafe dishing out meaty Texas chili and decadent pumpkin pancakes.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives: South of the Border
programme.desc: This trip, Guy's headed south of the border to Cabo San Lucas, Mexico. On the town's main drag, the taco joint that's turning out totally fresh octopus tacos and Guadalajaran-inspired sauces that'll knock your socks off.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives: Cosmopolitan Comfort
programme.desc: This trip, Guy Fieri's grabbing all kinds of cosmopolitan comfort food. In Portsmouth, New Hampshire, a righteous joint puts a New England spin on corned beef and cabbage, plus a fresh take on stroganoff.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives: Breakfast, Brisket and Belly
programme.desc: Guy is in Los Angeles at a late-night spot with a twist, Columbus for a joint serving creative combos like brisket on corn flakes and Phoenix at a barbeque stop with smoked prime rib and pork belly dumplings.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives: Multicultural Cooking
programme.desc: Guy digs into traditions from many cultures, including tortillas, tacos, Jewish deli food, and Puerto-Rican specialties.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives: Triple D Nation: Cultural Cookin'
programme.desc: Guy Fieri is getting culture shock, starting with a French-inspired Hawaiian hot spot transformed from a hole-in-the-wall lunch counter to a dine-in destination.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives: Triple D Nation: Layered, Stuffed and Stacked
programme.desc: It's a feeding frenzy on this trip, as Guy Fieri first stops in to see an old buddy in Kansas City, Mo., who's got new spots and a secret menu of sandwiches that are not so secret anymore.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives: Triple D Nation: East Coast Eatin'
programme.desc: On this trip, Guy Fieri checks in on some favorite East Coast eats. His first stop is a neighborhood joint that's still dishin' out diner classics from a cramped kitchen.

programme.title: The Pioneer Woman: Home Sweet Home: Brunch Bites
programme.desc: Ree Drummond is serving up brunch ideas that are perfect for the holidays and everyday. Her kids are the film crew as she takes four favorites to the next level, from a build-your-own leftover frittata sandwich board to a crispy a.m. wrap.

programme.title: The Pioneer Woman: New Year Brunch
programme.desc: Ree Drummond is gathering her friends for a New Year brunch featuring a menu of revamped favorites. In a fun twist on a classic, she makes everyone a baked bagel egg-in-a-hole topped with smoked salmon and more.

programme.title: The Pioneer Woman: Brunch Is in the Fridge!
programme.desc: Ree hosts brunch and makes things easy with a make-ahead spread. She gathers ingredients for a customizable bloody Mary, makes a tater tot casserole, fresh citrus salad with champagne vinegar dressing and pours some tropical bellinis for friends.

programme.title: The Pioneer Woman: Competing Breakfasts
programme.desc: Ree's making Ladd's favorite slap-up platter, the Farmer's Breakfast, with over easy fried eggs, a slab of honey-glazed ham, fried sausage and bacon.

programme.title: Girl Meets Farm: Brunch Club Strikes Again
programme.desc: It's Molly Yeh's turn to host brunch club with her friends again, so she makes blueberry cream cheese scones with yogurt glaze, bacon hasselback sweet potatoes, pita and greens benedict with feta cream and caraway bloody marys with mini reubens.

programme.title: Delicious Miss Brown: NOLA Brunch
programme.desc: Kardea Brown is whipping up a tasty brunch with a spicy twist. Her mom Pat is coming over for her favorite meal inspired by her top bucket list destination: New Orleans.

programme.title: Barefoot Contessa: Back to Basics: Ultimate Cocktails
programme.desc: Ina Garten is mixing up her favorite drinks for the cocktail hour, from spicy jalapeno margaritas to a new favorite cranberry martini. Plus, Ina has great ideas for scrumptious appetizers, such as lamb sausage in puff pastry and parmesan crisps.

programme.title: The Kitchen: New Year, New Brunch
programme.desc: The Kitchen is rolling out new flavours and fun twists on classic brunch staples for a New Year's brunch. Sunny makes breakfast steak and eggs with chimichurri. Then, the hosts share new takes on jam, jelly and marmalade.

programme.title: The Kitchen: Brunch Resolutions
programme.desc: The Kitchen kicks off the New Year with resolutions for brunch. Geoffrey Zakarian makes a brunch tortilla, Katie Lee creates pizza waffle sticks, Gesine Prado brings lemon blues muffins with crumble topping and Patrick Connolly shares banana French toast.

programme.desc: Tia Maria Torres works to save pit bulls from dangerous and abusive situations.

programme.title: Pit Bulls & Parolees: Sneak Peek
programme.desc: Tia Maria Torres works to save pit bulls from dangerous and abusive situations.

programme.desc: Tensions are high when Tia's husband faces a bail hearing after nearly 18 months of waiting, while a taxing adoption unfolds for a cast of renaissance fair pirates. Then, VRC must rescue a pack of abandoned wolf-dog hybrids at risk of being euthanized.

programme.desc: As hurricane season looms in New Orleans, Mariah and the twins are on a mission to find the owner of an abandoned Katrina dog. Meanwhile, Tia struggles to keep VRC afloat when the evacuation location floods.

programme.desc: Mariah is devastated when she learns that a friend has passed away.

programme.title: North Woods Law: Decoy Detail
programme.desc: Officer Egan sets up a decoy and checks hunters at a popular bird hunting site. Officers Towne and Bronson help rescue an injured hiker on Mount Kearsarge. Plus, Lt. Boudreau and Officer MacFadzen investigate a hunting on private property call.

programme.title: North Woods Law: Riverside Stakeout
programme.desc: Nature lovers show up for Discover Wild New Hampshire Day. Officers conduct an undercover surveillance. Officer McKee rescues a starving owl from the side of the road. Later on, Mount Monadnock, a perilous rescue of an injured hiker.

programme.title: North Woods Law: Last Days of Winter
programme.desc: The dive team assists at an event in Hampton Beach, while Officer Courtney patrols the area's clam flats. Officers Hannett and Holmes patrol snowmobile activity across the state. And Officer Demler helps a bobcat that's been injured by a porcupine.

programme.title: North Woods Law: Case Closed
programme.desc: Lt. Mike Eastman investigates a case of stolen lobster traps. A man on a dirt bike flees the scene after being stopped for speeding, and Officer Hannett sends an injured pigeon to a local wildlife rehabber.

programme.title: North Woods Law: Twists and Turns
programme.desc: Officer McDermott finds an abandoned truck, sparking a winding investigation. Officer Holmes and Sgt. Lucas rescue paddlers of a capsized canoe. Later, Holmes responds to a moose accident, while OFC. Frye consoles a bereft woman.

programme.title: North Woods Law: Stubborn as a Moose

programme.title: North Woods Law: Mountaintop Mystery
programme.desc: On Mt. Monadnock, a severed finger is found, sending a team and K-9 Wyatt to search for other possible body parts. A family of repeat offenders are found riding their ATVs without proper protective gear, and a man admits to an illegal bear kill.

programme.title: North Woods Law: Moose in the City

programme.title: 10 Things You Don't Know About: The Gold Rush

programme.title: Movie: Detour

programme.title: Big City Greens: Tilly Tour/ Dinner Party
programme.desc: Tilly takes Gramma out to be a tourist for a day. Cricket and Remy arrange a dinner party for everyone to get to know each other.

programme.desc: Cricket Green and his family move from the country to Big City to live with Gramma. Cricket and Bill accidentally disturb some raccoons in the garage, and they invade the house!

programme.desc: A report on the mysterious deaths inside a traveling commune and an investigation that spans 10 years. Two sisters and former commune members come forward for the first time to share their stories involving a diabolical world of lies, lust, and greed.

programme.title: Doctor Who: The Rebel Flesh
programme.desc: A solar tsunami liberates doppelgangers from their human 'originals' in a futuristic factory.

programme.title: Doctor Who: A Good Man Goes to War

programme.title: Doctor Who: The God Complex
programme.desc: The TARDIS lands in a hotel where every visitor's room contains their deepest, darkest fears.

programme.title: Doctor Who: Closing Time
programme.desc: The Doctor's final days are upon him, but when he drops in on an old friend, the Cybermen are waiting.

programme.title: Doctor Who: The Wedding of River Song
programme.desc: All of time and space hangs in balance by the shores of Lake Silencio in Utah; for this is the day the Doctor dies.

programme.title: Doctor Who: Asylum of the Daleks
programme.desc: The Doctor is kidnapped by an old foe and forced on an impossible mission to a place where even Daleks are too terrified to enter.

programme.title: Doctor Who: Dinosaurs on a Spaceship
programme.desc: An unmanned spaceship hurtles towards certain destruction unless the Doctor can save it and it's cargo of dinosaurs.

programme.title: Doctor Who: A Town Called Mercy

programme.title: Doctor Who: The Power of Three

programme.desc: As the weather begins to warm up, the staff at Pol Veterinary Services are thrown into a frenzy of frantic phone calls from clients needing their help.
